what_it_does: Renders a two-basin separator visualization for a Projection: points are placed left or right according to pole/lean, a balance bar shows the lean counts, and a textual verdict reports whether the projection separates.
observations: Uses framer-motion AnimatePresence with layoutId for animated decorative point circles.
Maintains a separate transparent SVG hit-layer with large touch targets for point selection.
Computes a diverging balance bar from lean_a and lean_b normalized by their sum.
Displays operator-driven pole labels as path leaves and offers a reset-to-default-poles button.
Draws a center line and faint basin centroid anchors to reinforce the two-gravity metaphor.
Point placement combines a horizontal sign from pole/lean with a vertical offset from theta.
standouts: Two-gravity basin coordinate mapping with left/right pole anchors.
Transparent hit-layer decoupled from animated decorative dots.
Conditional pole-label truncation and default-poles reset UI.
conventions: React functional component with local TypeScript type aliases.
CSS custom properties (variables) for colors and theming.
Custom useSize hook for measured SVG layout.
stamp utility applied to container and points for addressing/telemetry.
Motion transitions sourced from a shared tokens/motion module.
concerns: place() multiplies p.r by halfW without guarding against undefined p.r in this file.
Interactive points rely only on pointer click; no keyboard or ARIA role for selectable points.
Verdict and counts are rendered from optional fields without visible loading or missing-data state.
notes: A read-only, animated two-pole separator view that turns engine-reported lean and separation into a clickable basin diagram.
questions: What is the expected range and semantics of p.r and p.theta in ProjPoint?
How are polesDriven and onClearPoles managed by the parent, and what does default poles mean here?
What exact shape does proj.separation have at runtime, and are all fields always present?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose of showing a two-gravity separator form with real engine data matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Visualizing statistical separation as physical gravitational basins.
Minimal text overlays over a data-driven SVG canvas.
Operator override of binding poles with a path back to defaults.
intention_signals: Make the abstract notion of pole separation tangible by arranging points between two gravitational basins and summarizing the result as a clear yes/no verdict.
novelty: The two-basin coordinate system and diverging balance bar tied to a boolean separation verdict.
contribution: A concrete, animated two-pole separator visualization with point-selection hit targets, pole-label handling for operator-driven poles, and a clear separation verdict overlay.
summary_for_embedding: Separator.tsx is a React component that renders an animated two-basin separator visualization from a Projection, including point placement, a balance bar, pole labels, and a separates/does-not-separate verdict.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to let users visually inspect how a projection's points lean between two poles and whether those poles separate.

symbols.does: Declares a local type for lean counts and minority fraction.
symbols.description: Defines the Balance shape used inside Separation: counts for lean_a, lean_b, neutral, and a minority_frac number. It is a read-only data descriptor with no behavior.

symbols.does: Declares a local type for the engine's separation report.
symbols.description: Defines the Separation shape with optional separates boolean, optional balance, and optional pole_distinctness number. It mirrors the data expected from the projection engine.

symbols.code_id: code://company/surface/app/src/wheel/Separator.tsx::leaf | function | function leaf(s: string | undefined): string {
symbols.does: Returns the last path segment of a string with trailing slashes removed.
symbols.description: A small string utility used to shorten pole labels to their leaf name when poles are operator-driven, e.g., turning a full path into its final component.

symbols.does: Renders the two-gravity separator visualization and interaction surface.
symbols.description: Main React component that maps projection points into left/right basins, draws a center line, animated decorative dots, transparent hit targets, a diverging balance bar, pole labels, a reset-to-default-poles button, and a textual separation verdict.

symbols.does: Computes the SVG coordinates and basin sign for a projection point.
symbols.description: Maps a ProjPoint to an x/y position based on its pole or lean sign and its normalized theta angle, using the component's measured center and half-width/half-height.
